heading: Acquisition
label: 1132 Acquisition
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (e)(2), (3). added par. (2) and struck out former pars. (2) and (3) which read as follows:Pub. children.text: clearly defines the operational requirements for the project or program;
establishes the feasibility of alternatives;
develops an acquisition project or program baseline;
produces a life-cycle cost estimate; and
assesses the relative merits of alternatives to determine a preferred solution in accordance with the requirements of this section.
The key performance parameters, the key system attributes, and the operational performance attributes of the capability or asset to be acquired under the proposed acquisition project or program.
A detailed list of the systems or other capabilities with which the capability or asset to be acquired is intended to be interoperable, including an explanation of the attributes of interoperability.
The anticipated acquisition project or program baseline and acquisition unit cost for the capability or asset to be acquired under the project or program.

A detailed schedule for the acquisition process showing when all capability and asset acquisitions are to be completed and when all acquired capabilities and assets are to be initially and fully deployed.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Coast Guard may not acquire an experimental or technically immature capability or asset or implement a Level 1 or Level 2 acquisition project or program, unless it has prepared an analysis of alternatives for the capability or asset to be acquired in the concept and technology development phase of the acquisition process for the capability or asset.

children.label: A
children.text: an assessment of the technical maturity of the capability or asset, and technical and other risks;

children.label: B
children.text: an examination of capability, interoperability, and other advantages and disadvantages;

children.label: C
children.text: an evaluation of whether different combinations or quantities of specific assets or capabilities could meet the Coast Guard’s overall performance needs;

children.label: D
children.text: a discussion of key assumptions and variables, and sensitivity to change in such assumptions and variables;

children.label: E
children.text: when an alternative is an existing capability, asset, or prototype, an evaluation of relevant safety and performance records and costs;

children.label: i
children.text: an examination of likely research and development costs and the levels of uncertainty associated with such estimated costs;

children.label: ii
children.text: an examination of likely production and deployment costs and the levels of uncertainty associated with such estimated costs;

children.label: iii
children.text: an examination of likely operating and support costs and the levels of uncertainty associated with such estimated costs;

children.label: iv
children.text: if they are likely to be significant, an examination of likely disposal costs and the levels of uncertainty associated with such estimated costs; and

children.label: v
children.text: such additional measures as the Commandant or the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating determines to be necessary for appropriate evaluation of the capability or asset; and

children.label: G
children.text: the business case for each viable alternative.

children.heading: Test and Evaluation Master Plan.—
children.label: d Test and Evaluation Master Plan.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: For any Level 1 or Level 2 acquisition project or program the Chief Acquisition Officer must approve a test and evaluation master plan specific to the acquisition project or program for the capability, asset, or subsystems of the capability or asset and intended to minimize technical, cost, and schedule risk as early as practicable in the development of the project or program.

children.label: A
children.text: set forth an integrated test and evaluation strategy that will verify that capability-level or asset-level and subsystem-level design and development, including performance and supportability, have been sufficiently proven before the capability, asset, or subsystem of the capability or asset is approved for production; and

children.label: B
children.text: require that adequate developmental tests and evaluations and operational tests and evaluations established under subparagraph (A) are performed to inform production decisions.

children.label: A
children.text: the key performance parameters to be resolved through the integrated test and evaluation strategy;

children.label: B
children.text: the performance data to be used to determine whether the key performance parameters have been resolved;

children.label: C
children.text: critical operational issues to be assessed in addition to the key performance parameters;

children.label: D
children.text: the results during test and evaluation that will be required to demonstrate that a capability, asset, or subsystem meets performance requirements;

children.label: E
children.text: specific development test and evaluation phases and the scope of each phase;

children.label: F
children.text: modeling and simulation activities to be performed, if any, and the scope of such activities;

children.label: G
children.text: early operational assessments to be performed, if any, and the scope of such assessments;

children.label: H
children.text: operational test and evaluation phases;

children.label: I
children.text: an estimate of the resources, including funds, that will be required for all test, evaluation, assessment, modeling, and simulation activities; and

children.label: J
children.text: the Government entity or independent entity that will perform the test, evaluation, assessment, modeling, and simulation activities.

children.heading: .—Update
children.label: 4 .—Update
children.text: The Chief Acquisition Officer must approve an updated master plan whenever there is a revision to project or program test and evaluation strategy, scope, or phasing.

children.label: A
children.text: proceed beyond that phase of the acquisition process that entails approving the supporting acquisition of a capability or asset before the master plan is approved by the Chief Acquisition Officer; or

children.label: B
children.text: award any production contract for a capability, asset, or subsystem for which a master plan is required under this subsection before the master plan is approved by the Chief Acquisition Officer.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Commandant shall implement mechanisms to ensure the development and regular updating of life-cycle cost estimates for each acquisition with a total acquisition cost that equals or exceeds $10,000,000 and an expected service life of 10 or more years, and to ensure that these estimates are considered in decisions to develop or produce new or enhanced capabilities and assets.

children.label: i
children.text: each milestone decision is concluded; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the project or program enters a new acquisition phase; and

children.label: B
children.text: an independent cost estimate or independent cost assessment, as appropriate, be developed to validate life-cycle cost estimates developed under paragraph (1).
